summary: SnakeGame reads theme from DOM attribute instead of using the existing useSettingsStore().theme reactive ref
failure_scenario: App.vue, LockScreen.vue, and Settings.vue all use the settings store's reactive theme ref. SnakeGame is the only component reading document.documentElement.getAttribute('theme'). If the theme mechanism changes (e.g., attribute renamed to 'data-theme' or switched to a CSS class), the settings store's applyTheme() would be updated but SnakeGame would silently break. Additionally, reading the DOM attribute on every draw() call (every 150ms during gameplay) is unnecessary overhead compared to reading a reactive Pinia ref once.

summary: SnakeGame implements its own localStorage read/write with try/catch while category.ts has private loadFromStorage/saveToStorage helpers for the same pattern
failure_scenario: The project has four different localStorage access styles: category.ts wraps reads in try/catch (line 11), i18n/index.ts reads without try/catch (line 7), Settings.vue writes without try/catch (line 250), and SnakeGame wraps both read and write in try/catch (lines 98, 108). If the project later needs a shared localStorage feature (key prefix/namespace, migration, encryption, or a clear-all-data function), every one of these ad-hoc access points must be found and updated individually instead of changing one shared utility.
